name: Schwartz 10 broad basic values
description: Ten broad motivational value types from Schwartz's theory of basic human values.
citation: Schwartz, S. H. (1992). Universals in the content and structure of values. Advances in Experimental Social Psychology, 25, 1-65.
licenseNote: Names and original short paraphrases for educational personal use; no questionnaire items or scoring instrument included.

values.name: Self-direction
values.definition: Independent thought, choice, exploration, and creation.

values.name: Stimulation
values.definition: Novelty, challenge, and variety in life.

values.name: Hedonism
values.definition: Pleasure and sensuous gratification for oneself.

values.name: Achievement
values.definition: Demonstrating competence according to social standards.

values.name: Power
values.definition: Status, prestige, control, or dominance over resources and people.

values.name: Security
values.definition: Safety, harmony, and stability of self, relationships, and society.

values.name: Conformity
values.definition: Restraint of actions likely to upset others or violate expectations.

values.name: Tradition
values.definition: Respect for and commitment to inherited customs and ideas.

values.name: Benevolence
values.definition: Preserving and enhancing the welfare of close others.

values.name: Universalism
values.definition: Understanding, protecting, and promoting the welfare of all people and nature.
